purpose: Two systems in one file: (1) skin-color genetics — the paint-mixing inheritance model unique to Tunya. (2) cultural color symbolism — the meaning each color carries across kingdoms.

axioms: Most children adopt a mixture of their parents' skin colors. Yellow + blue parents → green child. Red + blue → purple. Yellow + red → orange. Blends are real hue arithmetic, not melanin gradient.

Throwback rule: sometimes a child takes one parent's coloring directly instead of blending. Sex-linked tendency — girls usually take their mother's coloring, boys usually take their father's. The tendency is statistical, not absolute.

After 4 generations of intermixing across arks, Ancient Tunyans, Medici, and Sanguire, Tunya's people span every color on the spectrum. Pure single-hue individuals are rare; blended hues (teal, mauve, olive, ochre, slate, pearl-rose, ash-violet) dominate.
Skin color carries genealogical information. A skilled observer can read parentage from a child's hue.

throwback_sex_linkage.note: Tendency, not rule. The 35/65 split keeps the model probabilistic and avoids deterministic outcomes.

implementation.purity_decay: After 4 generations of mixing, the probability of any pure single-hue child is below 5% in most kingdoms. Pure-color individuals are visibly distinctive.

species_origin_palettes.note: These are species-origin palettes. Most Tunyans 4G are blends across multiple species. The procgen NPC generator should sample parents' colors from kingdom-specific bias and produce blended children.

kingdom_palette_priors.comment: Per-kingdom bias toward subsets of the full Tunyan spectrum, reflecting historical settlement patterns. Sample parent colors with these priors; child colors emerge from the genetics engine.

narrative_implications: An NPC's skin color is a readable diplomatic credential. A teal-skinned diplomat in Akeia is visibly Sahm-Bahiij descended; the queen mother knows what bloodlines stand at her court before any introduction.

Pure single-hue individuals are visibly distinctive. A pure Sanguire-red character in Dormas is unusual enough to be commented on. A pure pearl Medici walking Sahm's market is recognizable at distance.

Throwback children — taking one parent's color directly — are biological reminders of lineage purity. Some kingdoms (Nil, Sandrun) value them; others (Dormas, Bahiij) treat them as quaintly old-fashioned.

Cross-species blends produce species-distinctive shades — pearl-rose for Medici-Sanguire, ash-violet for Medici-Tunyan, ember-brown for Sanguire-Tunyan. Skilled observers identify rare hybrids by hue alone.

color_symbolism.policy: Each color carries multiple meanings, sometimes contradictory. Context determines reading. Faction cultures stake their identity on specific color readings — a yellow banner on a battlefield reads as either provocation or surrender depending on who flies it.

yellow.battlefield_ambiguity: A yellow banner on a battlefield reads either as provocation or as surrender. Bahiij has flown both meanings within living memory.

pearl_white.note: Not in the original color table but added per the species-origin palette. Pearl-white is the Medici's signature.

diplomatic_color_reading.amity_courtship_ball_akeia: Foreign delegates wear their kingdom's primary color. Akeia hosts read it: a Sahm delegate in red-trim is signaling alliance interest; in plain river-iron yellow is signaling polite distance.
diplomatic_color_reading.harvest_high_feast_aekon: All twelve districts wear their district color. The twelfth wears no color — its dish is on the table; its presence is felt; its color is a refusal.
diplomatic_color_reading.cull_sanguire_warlord_challenge: Challenger wears the color of their clan; warlord wears Sanguire-red. Spectators wear neutral browns. A spectator in clan-color is making a political statement and may be challenged in turn.
diplomatic_color_reading.cares_medici_holiday_communion: Medici procession is pure pearl-white. Foreign witnesses must wear muted neutrals. A foreigner in any saturated color on Cares-day-1 is a recorded insult.

character_skin_color_engine.description: Sample parent skin colors from the kingdom_palette_priors of the parents' kingdoms (cross-kingdom unions sample from both). Apply the genetics blend_function. Apply the throwback_probability + sex-linkage. Apply the jitter. Output the child's RGB.

edge_cases.pure_species_throwback: If both parents are flagged pure_<species>, the throwback probability rises to 0.40 (the species-internal palette is more genetically stable).
edge_cases.cross_species_blend: If parents are different species, label the child's hue with the species-blend tag (e.g. 'pearl_rose' for Medici-Sanguire, 'ash_violet' for Medici-Tunyan, 'ember_brown' for Sanguire-Tunyan).

faction_banner_generator.description: When generating a faction's banner SVG, prioritize the faction's primary_color from factions.json AND respect the cultural meanings the faction stakes its identity on (e.g. Sahm river-iron yellow specifically reads as the kingdom's optimistic-without-naivety stance, not as cowardice).
faction_banner_generator.color_meaning_filter: filter the symbolism table for the faction's stated cultural alignment before rendering tooltip text on banners in-game
